No Poverty.reason: The course's focus on 'risk management' and 'liability insurance' can be inferred to support social protection systems (Target 1.3) and build resilience against shocks (Target 1.5), directly linking to poverty reduction.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to food security, agriculture, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The study of 'liability insurance' directly relates to financial risk protection (Target 3.8) and 'risk management' is crucial for managing national and global health risks (Target 3.d).

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly aims to develop 'mature legal research ability' and 'research capabilities,' directly aligning with quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and relevant skills (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: While 'social responsibility' is mentioned, there is no specific evidence or strong inference in the syllabus directly addressing gender equality or related issues.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on 'insurance' strengthens financial institutions (Target 8.10), and 'risk management' can promote safe working environments (Target 8.8) and economic stability.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Developing 'research capabilities' enhances scientific research (Target 9.5), and 'risk management' knowledge is vital for resilient infrastructure and sustainable industries (Targets 9.1, 9.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Legal research methods taught in the course can be applied to study and eliminate discriminatory laws and policies (Target 10.3), indirectly contributing to reduced inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course's emphasis on 'risk management' and 'liability insurance' is directly relevant to managing economic losses from disasters and building community resilience (Targets 11.5, 11.b).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core competency B explicitly states students will 'understand and identify... sustainability issues,' directly linking to encouraging sustainable practices and reporting (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: The study of 'risk management' and 'insurance' is integral to strengthening resilience and adaptive capacity to climate-related hazards and natural disasters (Target 13.1).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: As a 'Research Methods of Law' course, it fundamentally promotes the rule of law (Target 16.3) and develops effective, accountable institutions (Target 16.6) through legal and comparative research.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's 'comparative law' component fosters international cooperation (Target 17.6), and statistical methods support data for measuring progress (Target 17.19).
